reasoning: Google Chrome holds a commanding lead in global browser market share, capturing over 65% of the market (and upwards of ~71.86% on desktop) as of late 2025. Safari is a distant second, hovering around 18%. Market shares in this space are famously stable and it takes years to see a small percentage swing in usage organically. The only conceivable black swan event that could disrupt Chrome's #1 spot by January 1, 2026, would be a catastrophic forced divestment/rebranding stemming from the current DOJ antitrust suit. However, ongoing legal proceedings, including DOJ remedy proposals, appeals, and multi-year implementation timelines (estimated at 5-10 years for a technical transition), guarantee that Google Chrome as an entity/name will not be dismantled or wiped from browser ranking lists like StatCounter/Wikipedia by the end of 2025. The market correctly priced this at 0.97, and I am highly confident in this estimate.
